title: Cyathostemma excelsum
taxonomy: Cyathostemma excelsum is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. This family is part of the order Gentianales, which also includes families such as Asclepiadaceae and Loganiaceae. The order Gentianales is a subset of the larger clade Asterids in the Angiosperm Phylogeny Group (APG) III classification. The family Apocynaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, including Periplocoideae and Plumerioideae. Cyathostemma excelsum's exact classification may also be influenced by ongoing research and updates in plant taxonomy.

intro: Cyathostemma excelsum, also known as the mountain cycas or Cycas excelsum, is a species of cycad, a type of seed plant that has been around for over 200 million years. The health benefits of Cyathostemma excelsum include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include cycasin, a toxic compound that has been found to have anticancer properties, as well as other compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids that contribute to its medicinal properties. Cyathostemma excelsum is often used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal benefits.
